DECLARATION OF (REPLY) JOHN CU IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINITFF (TRANSACTION ID # 62754933) FILED BY DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO INSURANCE SERVICES USA, INC KLASS, DUSTIN GRAY, DONALD G.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO INSURANCE SERVICES USA, INC, DUSTIN KLASS, DONALD GRAY'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF. GRANTED. THE TRUST DOCUMENTS ARE RELEVANT. PLAINTIFF, AS PARTY CLAIMING THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE, HAS THE BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THE PRELIMINARY FACTS NECESSARY TO SUPPORT ITS CLAIM. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRODUCED A PRIVILEGE LOG OR SUFFICIENT FACTUAL INFORMATION FOR OTHER PARTIES TO EVALUATE THE MERITS OF THAT CLAIM. CCP 2031.240(C),(1). WITHIN 20 DAYS, PLAINTIFF SHALL PRODUCE A PRIVILEGE LOG WITH SUFFICIENT DETAILS AS TO THE UNDERLYING INFORMATION TO MEET THE PRELIMINARY FACTUAL SHOWING REQUIRED TO SUPPORT THE PRIVILEGE CLAIM; OTHERWISE, PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE AMENDED CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES AND DOCUMENTS. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT AARON@MINNISANDSMALLETS.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response And Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO INSURANCE SERVICES USA, INC, DUSTIN KLASS, DONALD GRAY's Motion To Compel Further Response And Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff.

Granted. The trust documents are relevant. Plaintiff, as party claiming the attorney-client privilege, has the burden of establishing the preliminary facts necessary to support its claim. Plaintiff has not met his burden because plaintiff has not produced a privilege log or sufficient factual information for other parties to evaluate the merits of that claim. CCP 2031.240(c),(1). Within 20 days, plaintiff shall produce a privilege log with sufficient details as to the underlying information to meet the preliminary factual showing required to support the privilege claim; otherwise, plaintiff shall provide amended Code-compliant responses and documents. Sanctions are denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by email to the Judge Pro Tem at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-15-2018 Continued To Dec-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lee J. Novich, Esq. 415-537-3900 of the Construction Law Group for Plaintiff Clare Springs Trustee.
Kaylen Kadotani, Esq. 415-777-3200 of Hanson bridgett for Defendant Wells Fargo.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues and request severance. Defendant has no contact with Mr. Craig. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO INSURANCE SERVICES USA, INC, DUSTIN KLASS, DONALD GRAY'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

The tentative ruling is revised. The Court grants Defendants' motion for leave to file a cross-complaint. The current trial date is retained. The parties are to return on 12/5/18 at 9:30 am to update the Court on efforts to serve Mr. Craig, to consider severance and whether the trial date should be continued or not.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
